title: De-dup key omits round/stage, can merge distinct cup fixtures
evidence: src/model.rs:53 dedup_key() = (competition, season, home_key, away_key); src/data.rs:54 push() skips any repeat. Correct for league round-robins but two distinct Copa do Brasil / Libertadores meetings with the same ordered pairing in one season collapse to one.
suggestion: Include round/stage (and ideally date) in dedup_key, or scope de-dup to the league files where cross-file overlap actually occurs.

title: Team query falls back to substring containment, can over-match
evidence: src/teams.rs:204 query_matches returns stored_key.contains(&q) || q.contains(stored_key); a query like 'America' (not in the alias table) matches both 'América-MG' and 'América-RN'.
suggestion: Document the partial-match semantics, or require exact canonical match when the query resolves to a known alias.

title: Unit-TDD layer is thin relative to the ATDD prompt
evidence: Inline #[test] only in src/normalize.rs (3) and src/teams.rs (3); src/data.rs and src/tools.rs have no unit tests and are covered only by the acceptance suite.
suggestion: Add unit tests for CSV parsing/de-dup in data.rs and aggregation edge cases in tools.rs to satisfy the 'finer-grained unit TDD underneath' instruction more fully.

title: Acceptance tests run against the fixed corpus, not a per-test 'empty system'
evidence: tests/common/mod.rs:27 spawns the real binary which loads all of data/kaggle/; assertions pin real values (Flamengo 2019 = 90 pts). The ATDD prompt's literal 'empty system with per-test data' isn't applied.
suggestion: Acceptable for a read-only query server over a provided corpus; the independence/isolation intent (fresh process per test, no shared mutable state) is met. No change required.

title: Date-range filter silently drops matches with no parseable date
evidence: src/tools.rs:108-109 start_date/end_date filters require !m.date.is_empty(); BR-Football rows with unparseable dates are excluded from date-bounded queries.
suggestion: Document that date-range queries only return dated fixtures; consider surfacing an undated-count in the response.
